FUN-1: The model describes the Bakery algorithm for mutual exclusion between processes.
EQP-1: Processes are represented as a set, and each process has a unique identifier given by a function Id.
EQP-2: The maximum cycle count is a natural number that limits how many actions a process may perform.
FUN-2: Each process may perform the following actions: request to start execution, request a ticket, enter the critical section if conditions allow, leave the critical section, or fail and stop execution.
FUN-3: Ticket assignment follows the Bakery principle: a process receives a strictly positive ticket number greater than all currently active tickets.
SAF-1: At any given time, at most one process is in the critical section, ensuring mutual exclusion.
SAF-2: Any process in the critical section must hold a valid ticket number that is non-zero.
SAF-3: If two processes both request entry, the one with the smaller ticket number will be granted entry first; if ticket numbers are equal, the one with the smaller Id enters first.
